invariant: ADOPT Stop condition (clean path) — writes .aprd/aprd.frozen.md + .hld/skeleton.lock + .adr/log/NNNN-*.md stubs. Omitting skeleton.lock = incomplete output; the ADP foundation is unusable without a structural lock.
owning_role: ADOPT (00-aprd/ADOPT.md) — Step 7 writes .hld/skeleton.lock; omitting it violates the output contract (outputs[1]).
seed: run ADOPT against src/ bench; remove .hld/skeleton.lock from the output before asserting (simulates runner that wrote aprd.frozen.md + .adr/ stubs but skipped skeleton.lock)
load_bearing_assertion: Golden ADOPT output has .hld/skeleton.lock present (artifact: skeleton.lock, class: adopt-bootstrap, components[] non-empty). Defect output is missing .hld/skeleton.lock entirely. Done-sentinel check: skeleton.lock absent = output contract violated.
separates_from_golden_by: .hld/skeleton.lock file presence (golden: present with components / defect: absent). The structural stub is a mandatory ADOPT output — its absence is an incomplete adoption that blocks normal ADP intake.
e2e_validated: 2026-06-12 — golden run produces .hld/skeleton.lock (class:adopt-bootstrap, 4 components, 3 seams). Defect: removing skeleton.lock from output leaves .hld/ empty. Verifier checks for skeleton.lock existence and class discriminator; absence = FAIL.
